description: Google OAuth Sign-In - Gate /interview access behind Google authentication, auto-fill student name, show authenticated users on instructor dashboard

userStories.title: Add JWT utility and session secret environment variable
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared utility for signing and verifying JWTs so the auth functions can create and validate session tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create netlify/functions/lib/jwt.js with signJwt(payload) and verifyJwt(token) functions using the jsonwebtoken npm package
JWT is signed with SESSION_SECRET env var, expires in 8 hours
Add jsonwebtoken to package.json dependencies
Add SESSION_SECRET to .env.example (not .env) as a placeholder
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a foundational utility used by auth-callback (US-003) and auth-session (US-004). Keep it minimal — just sign and verify.

userStories.title: Create auth-login Netlify Function to initiate Google OAuth flow
userStories.description: As a student, I need an endpoint that redirects me to Google's consent screen so I can sign in with my Google account.
acceptanceCriteria: Netlify Function at netlify/functions/auth-login.js handles GET requests
Constructs Google OAuth 2.0 authorization URL with client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope=openid+profile+email
Reads GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID from environment variables
Accepts an optional 'redirect' query param and passes it through as OAuth state parameter (for preserving ?code= workshop session code)
Returns 302 redirect to the Google authorization URL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The redirect_uri should point to /.netlify/functions/auth-callback. The state param preserves the original URL the student was trying to reach.

userStories.title: Create auth-callback Netlify Function to handle OAuth response
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that exchanges Google's authorization code for tokens, extracts the user profile, and sets a session cookie.
acceptanceCriteria: Netlify Function at netlify/functions/auth-callback.js handles GET requests
Exchanges the authorization code for tokens by POSTing to https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
Extracts name, email, and picture from the Google ID token (decode JWT payload) or calls the userinfo endpoint
Creates a signed JWT containing name, email, and picture using the jwt utility from US-001
Sets an HTTP-only, Secure, SameSite=Lax cookie named 'session' with the JWT, max-age 28800 (8 hours)
Redirects to /interview (or to the URL from the state parameter if present, preserving query params like ?code=)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET env vars. The redirect_uri must match what was used in auth-login.

userStories.title: Create auth-session Netlify Function to check authentication status
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint that returns the current user's profile if authenticated or 401 if not, so the frontend can check auth state.
acceptanceCriteria: Netlify Function at netlify/functions/auth-session.js handles GET requests
Parses the 'session' cookie from the request headers
Verifies the JWT using the jwt utility from US-001
If valid, returns 200 with JSON body: { authenticated: true, user: { name, email, picture } }
If missing or invalid, returns 401 with JSON body: { authenticated: false }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create auth-logout Netlify Function
userStories.description: As a student, I need an endpoint that clears my session cookie so I can sign out.
acceptanceCriteria: Netlify Function at netlify/functions/auth-logout.js handles POST requests
Clears the 'session' cookie by setting it with max-age=0 and the same path/domain attributes
Returns 200 with JSON body: { success: true }
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create the /login page with Google sign-in button
userStories.description: As a student, I want a login page with a 'Sign in with Google' button so I can authenticate before accessing the workshop.
acceptanceCriteria: SvelteKit route exists at src/routes/login/+page.svelte
/login page displays the application name/branding and a brief instruction ('Sign in to join the workshop')
Page has a 'Sign in with Google' button styled per Google branding guidelines (Google 'G' logo, 'Sign in with Google' text)
Clicking the button navigates to /.netlify/functions/auth-login (passing the current redirect URL if a ?redirect param exists)
On mount, page calls /.netlify/functions/auth-session — if already authenticated, redirects to /interview automatically
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Keep the page simple and focused. Use existing app styles/colors for consistency.

userStories.title: Protect the /interview route with auth redirect
userStories.description: As the system, I need to redirect unauthenticated users from /interview to /login so only signed-in students can access the workshop.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a +layout.server.js (or +page.server.js) for the /interview route that checks the session cookie
If the session cookie is missing or JWT is invalid, redirect to /login?redirect=/interview (preserving any query params like ?code=SESSION_CODE)
If authenticated, pass the user profile (name, email, picture) to the page via the load function
After successful login, the student is redirected back to /interview with their original query params intact
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The JWT verification should happen server-side in the load function. Import the jwt utility or decode inline since this runs in SvelteKit server context.

userStories.title: Auto-fill student name from Google profile and skip name entry
userStories.description: As a student, I want my name automatically filled from my Google account so I skip the manual name entry step.
acceptanceCriteria: The authenticated user's Google display name is passed from the layout/page load data to the interview page
On the interview page, if the user is authenticated and has a Google name, skip the EntryScreen name input step
The Google display name is stored in localStorage as ws_studentName for consistency with the existing flow
The name appears correctly in room picker, waiting screen, and interview screen
The EntryScreen still shows for the session code input step (only name entry is skipped)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The existing flow uses ws_studentName in localStorage throughout. Set it from the Google profile so all downstream components work unchanged.

userStories.title: Store authenticated user identity in Netlify Blobs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to persist each authenticated user's Google profile server-side so the instructor dashboard can show who signed in.
acceptanceCriteria: In auth-callback, after successful authentication, store the user profile in Netlify Blobs under key user:{email}
Stored data includes: name, email, picture (avatar URL), and authenticatedAt timestamp
In workshop-join, when a student joins a room, add their email to the room blob's students entry if authenticated
The email field is read from the session cookie JWT in workshop-join
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: workshop-join already receives the student name. Now it also needs to parse the session cookie to get the email and associate it with the student in the room.

userStories.title: Display authenticated students on instructor dashboard
userStories.description: As an instructor, I want to see which students have authenticated and their Google account info on my dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Instructor dashboard shows a list/table of authenticated students for the current session
Each entry displays: student name, email, circular avatar thumbnail, and sign-in time
Only students who have both authenticated AND joined a session room are shown
List updates on dashboard refresh or poll cycle
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The dashboard already polls for room data. Extend the room data response to include student emails, then look up user:{email} blobs for avatar and sign-in time.

userStories.title: Add sign-out button to the interview page
userStories.description: As a student, I want a sign-out button so I can switch Google accounts or end my authenticated session.
acceptanceCriteria: A small, unobtrusive 'Sign out' button is visible in the top-right corner of the /interview page
Clicking it calls /.netlify/functions/auth-logout (POST)
After logout, clears ws_studentName and other ws_* localStorage keys
Redirects the user to /login
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add loading spinner between Google redirect and interview page
userStories.description: As a student, I want visual feedback while my session is being established after signing in with Google.
acceptanceCriteria: After the auth-callback redirect lands on /interview, a brief loading/spinner screen is shown while the auth-session check completes
The spinner displays a message like 'Signing you in...'
Once auth is confirmed, the spinner transitions to the normal interview flow (EntryScreen for session code)
If auth check fails, redirect to /login
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This may already be handled naturally by the layout load function from US-007. Only add if there's a visible delay between redirect and page render.
